translations.text: he/she doesn’t listen to me
if on the 15th day the Moon-god dies (= eclipses), the Great-King will die
he/she closes the gate
If the Moon-god hasn’t yet risen
They will restore the old ritual for old Ištar
And he/she closes / will close the door
If/when the gods acknowledge / recognize me
The chief of the cooks libates into the fireplace once
The deaf man closes / will close the windows
I offer / will offer one cow and 48 sheep to the deity
(But) when I arrived in Tarkuma, I burnt Tarkuma down to the ground.
The enemy did not flee.
He fled away before me
He fled away from Maraššantii̯a
And you will not show them the road to Ḫatti-land
Telipinu chased them away
But/and we will attack (the city of) Kapapaḫšuu̯a
I have just interrogated them
My Majesty has ordered Kallu as the Lord of the Cavalry
But then the enemy attacked (the city of) Zikkatta
The gods have allotted many years to me, the King
The Fate-Goddess has treated you badly
The King is sitting in/on the wagon
He is hit by the Storm-god
The King cleansed / purified himself

text: kat-ta-ma tu-el DUMU^MEŠ^-_KA am-me-el DUMU-_I̯A pa-aḫ-ša-ri
title: Example 3.6

translations.text: But/and further, my son will protect your sons
A heavy kunkunuzzi-stone fell down from the sky

text: ma-a-an-kán an-tu-uḫ-ša-aš la-ga-a-ri na-aš-ma-aš-kán ^GIŠ^GIGIR-az kat-ta ma-a-uš-zi
title: Example 3.8

translations.text: If a person falls over, or (if) (s)he falls down from a wagon
You have become a wolf

text: ma-aḫ-ḫa-an-ma-za _A-BU-I̯A ^m^mur-ši-li-iš DINGIR^LIM^-iš ki-ša-at ŠEŠ-I̯A-ma-za-kán ^m^NIR.GÁL _A-NA ^GIŠ^GU.ZA _A-BI-ŠU e-ša-at am-mu-uk-ma-za _A-NA _PA-NI ŠEŠ-I̯A EN.KARAŠ ki-iš-ḫa-ḫa-at
title: Example 3.10

translations.text: (But) when my father Muršili became a god (= died), my brother Muu̯atalli sat down on the throne of his father, I became head of the army for (in service of) my brother
He/she is heard / is being listened to
Then they made them blind.
And we will take care of the gods on the ritual feast.

text: nu-za ^URU^pal-ḫu-iš-ša-an-na tar-aḫ-ḫu-un nu URU-an ar-ḫa u̯a-ar-nu-nu-un
title: Example 3.14

translations.text: I conquered (the city of) Palḫuišša as well, and I burnt the city down to the ground

text: ma-a-an-mu DINGIR^MEŠ^ ka-ni-iš-ša-an-zi LUGAL-ez-zi-aḫ-ḫa-ri tu-uk-ma am-me-ta-az SIG5-eš-zi
title: Example 3.15

translations.text: when Gods elect me and I will become king it will become good to you from me
You will eat bread and drink water
They took each other by the hand
It does not lie on this tablet of the oath

text: ku-it-ma-an-za ú-e-eš nu-u-a ^URU^ḫa-at-tu-ši e-šu-en LÚ^MEŠ^ ^URU^KA4-AŠ-KA4-ma iš-ta-ma-aš-ša-an-zi
title: Example 4.04

translations.text: While we were still present in Ḫattuša, the Kaškaeans were (litt. “are”) listening

text: nu-un-na-aš an-ni-ša-an-pát na-ak-ki-e-eš a-aš-ša-u-e-eš e-šu-u-en nu-un-na-aš le-en-ki-i̯a-aš e-šu-u-en
title: Example 4.05

translations.text: In the past we were important and good, we were (men) of the oath

text: ma-a-an ^URU^ḫa-at-tu-ši šal-li-iš u̯a-aš-ta-a-iš ki-ša-ri na-aš-šu-za LUGAL-uš na-aš-ma MUNUS.LUGAL-aš DINGIR^LIM^-iš ki-ša-ri
title: Example 4.06

translations.text: If in Ḫattuša a large disaster will take place, (i.e., if) either the king of or the queen will become a god (= dies)
(But) our head spins like a potter’s wheel

text: ^d^UTU-uš dam-me-iš-ḫa-an-da-aš ku-ri-im-ma-aš-ša an-tu-uḫ-ḫa-aš at-ta-aš an-na-aš zi-ik
title: Example 4.08

translations.text: You, Sun-god, are father (and) mother for suppressed and bereft persons
And I will eat you fully
Now I will eat and drink
(But) in Kaška, the locust have eaten the grain (harvests)
If, however, you will protect (= obey) the words of this tablet
Why don’t you eat nor drink?
The king will offer to the gods (specifically) these ones thirteen times through the window

text: nu LUGAL-uš a-ki _I-NA ^GIŠ^GU.ZA-ŠU-ma-za-kán ta-ma-iš ku-iš-ki e-ša-ri
title: Example 4.15

translations.text: The king will die, and someone else will sit down on his throne

text: ták-ku LÚ-aš da-me-da-ni A.ŠÀ A.GÀR an-da a-ki
title: Example 4.16

translations.text: If a person dies inside another field...
They have renewed his years and his awe
My father brought us together and made us swear an oath

text: nu KUR ^URU^pár-ḫa-an-na _IŠ-TU ^GIŠ^TUKUL e-ep-zi nu-kán a-pa-a-aš-ša _A-NA LUGAL KUR ^URU^ ^d^U-TA-AŠ-ŠA a-aš-ša-an-za
title: Example 5.03

translations.text: He will take the Land of Parḫa, too, with weapons, and that (land) too will then be belonging to the King of the Land of Tarḫuntašša.
They seized him and they killed (him) together with his sons

text: _A-BU-I̯A-ma-an-na-aš-za ^m^mur-ši-li-iš 4 DUMU^ḪI.A^ ^m^ḫal-pa-šu-lu-pí-in ^m^NIR.GÁL-in ^m^ḫa-at-tu-ši-li-in ^f^DINGIR^MEŠ^.IR-in-na DUMU.MUNUS-an ḫa-aš-ta nu-za ḫu-u-ma-an-da-aš-pát EGIR-ez-zi-iš DUMU-aš e-šu-un
title: Example 5.05

translations.text: My father Muršili fathered us, four children, Ḫalpašulupi, Muu̯attalli, Ḫattušili, and Maššannauzzi, a daughter. And I was the youngest child of all.

text: nu-mu ^d^IŠTAR GAŠAN-I̯A ku-it ka-ni-iš-ša-an ḫar-ta ŠEŠ-I̯A-i̯a-mu ^m^NIR.GÁL a-aš-šu ḫar-ta
title: Example 5.06

translations.text: Because Ishtar, My Lady, had recognized / elected me, my brother Muwatalli held me dear.
Now (s)he will stand / stands on / near the altar
I will either kill them or deport them

text: ki-i _ṬUP-PU ar-ḫa ḫar-ra-an e-eš-ta na-at _A-NA _PA-NI ^m^_MA-AḪ-ḪU-ZI _Ù _A-NA ^m^ḪAL-U̯A-LÚ ú-uk ^m^du-da-aš EGIR-pa ne-u̯a-aḫ-ḫu-un
title: Example 5.09

translations.text: This tablet was fully destroyed. And I, Duda, made it new again before the eyes of Mahhuzi and Halwaziti

text: nu É.DINGIR^LIM^ im-ma 1-an ḫar-ak-zi ^URU^ḫa-at-tu-ša-aš-ma LUGAL-aš a-aš-šu _UL ḫar-ak-zi
title: Example 5.10

translations.text: One temple indeed will perish but the possessions of the King of Hattuša will not perish
They destroyed all of it fully!

text: pár-ku-i-ša-aš a-pa-a-aš mi-iš-ri-u̯a-an-za-aš a-pa-a-aš ḫar-ki-ša-aš a-pa-a-aš na-aš-kán ḫu-u-ma-an-da-az a-ša-nu-u̯a-an-za
title: Example 5.12

translations.text: She is pure, that one; she is beautiful, that one; she is white, that one, and she is provided with everything
(But) afterwards / then the enemy killed him
They sweep the temples
(But) Kupanta-LAMMA has sworn these things opposite / in the presence of / standing before Madduwatta
